commit.message:
refactor: separate NES-specific code from generic emulator platform (#1884) (#1897)

* refactor(#1884): move NES-specific modules to src/nes/

Move cpu/, ppu/, apu/, bus/, cartridge/, console/, and integration_tests/
into src/nes/ subdirectory to establish a clear boundary between
NES-specific code and generic platform infrastructure.

Add pub use re-exports in lib.rs and main.rs so all existing
crate::cpu, crate::ppu, etc. paths continue to work unchanged.

Fix hardcoded file paths:
- rom_db.csv path in RomDb::new() and include_str! in app_context.rs
- Mapper registry source path in integration test

All 6739 lib tests pass. No behavior changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(#1884): add Console enum with hardware-agnostic interface

Create src/emulator.rs with Console enum wrapping Nes, providing a
common interface for frontends: run_tick, render, audio, input,
save/load state, reset, and ROM loading. System-specific features
remain accessible via pattern matching on the Console::Nes variant.

Includes 14 unit tests covering all Console methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(#1884): split Config into FrontendConfig + NesConfig

Extract generic frontend settings (audio, video, autorun, debugger UI,
window settings) into FrontendConfig in src/config.rs. NES-specific
hardware settings (hardware_mode, expansion_port, APU channels, RAM init,
etc.) stay as NesConfig in src/nes/console/config.rs.

Config is now a composition: Config { frontend: FrontendConfig, nes: NesConfig }.
AutorunMode enum moves to src/config.rs as it's system-agnostic.

All 6753 tests pass unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#1884): fix remaining config field access patterns for native/wasm features

Update multi-line .config().hardware_model patterns to .config().nes.hardware_model
in wasm.rs, event_loop.rs, and app_state.rs. Fix Config struct literal in main.rs
to use NesConfig nesting, and update .config().rom_path to .config().frontend.rom_path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(#1884): migrate native frontend event loop to use Console enum

NativeEventLoop now stores Console instead of Nes. Common operations
(reset, save_ram, screen_crc32, joypad state, clear_ready_to_render)
go through the Console interface. NES-specific operations (keyboard,
mouse, gamepad, debugger, rendering) use inline destructuring to
extract the inner Nes, allowing Rust's borrow checker to split borrows
on struct fields correctly.

Added Console methods: app_context(), save_ram(), set_audio_sample_rate().
Updated main.rs to create Console::new_nes() and pass it to the event loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(#1884): remove pub use re-exports, update all import paths to crate::nes::

Replace ~2036 import paths from crate::{console,cpu,ppu,apu,bus,cartridge}
to crate::nes::{console,cpu,ppu,apu,bus,cartridge} across 304 files.
Remove backward-compat pub use re-exports from lib.rs and main.rs.
Update bin/roms.rs external import to neser::nes::cartridge.

Add cfg(test) GameBoy stub in emulator.rs to validate the Console enum
can dispatch across multiple hardware targets (2 new tests).

Update architecture.md to reflect the new layered architecture:
Console enum, src/nes/ namespace, FrontendConfig/NesConfig split,
native frontend (winit+GL), and corrected directory paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#1884): fix neser::cartridge → neser::nes::cartridge in bin/roms.rs tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(#1884): address PR review comments

- Remove redundant app_context param from Console::load_rom; use internal clone
- Add Nes::SCREEN_WIDTH/SCREEN_HEIGHT constants; use in Console instead of magic numbers
- Add debug_assert for invalid button_id in Console::set_button
- Add Nes::set_audio_sample_rate to avoid abstraction leak through APU internals
- Replace MultiConsole with trait-based SystemOps proving extensibility at compile time

fix(autoupdate): set UpdateStatus based on NupkgPath, add package filter for workflow_dispatch

Previously, UpdateStatus was set to 'Update Available' when AU detected a newer remote version,
even if no .nupkg was built (e.g. version already exists in Chocolatey community feed).
This caused 'dotnet nuget push' to fail with a null path error.

Fix: derive UpdateStatus from NupkgPath so push steps only run when a package was actually built.

Also adds a package input to workflow_dispatch so individual packages can be tested manually.

commit.message:
fix: bearer token creation truncates client_name and returns wrong expiry for existing tokens (#22167)

* Initial plan

* fix: bearer token creation returns wrong expiry and truncates client_name

Two bugs in src/web/api/http_auth.c:

1. bearer_create_token_internal: strncpyz used sizeof(bt->cloud_account_id)
   (16 bytes) instead of sizeof(bt->client_name) (64 bytes) when storing
   the client name, silently truncating it to 15 characters. This broke the
   dedup logic in bearer_create_token (strncmp would never match names
   longer than 15 chars) and saved incorrect data to on-disk token files.

2. bearer_create_token: when an existing matching token was found in the
   in-memory dictionary, the function returned the local `expires_s`
   variable (initialized to 0) instead of bt->expires_s (the actual token
   expiration). This caused bearer_get_token_json_response to return
   "expiration": 0 (Unix epoch) to the dashboard, making the dashboard
   believe the bearer token had already expired even though it was valid.

fix(order-processor): narrow exception handler and add docstrings (H-01, L-01)

## Summary
This commit resolves two findings from architecture review:
- **H-01 (High)**: Broad exception handler in _parse_expiry() masked programming errors
- **L-01 (Low)**: Missing docstrings reduced code maintainability

## Changes
1. **Narrowed exception handler** (_parse_expiry):
   - Changed: except Exception as exc: → except (ValueError, IndexError) as exc:
   - Reason: Only ValueError and IndexError can occur from split() and date().
   - Benefit: Unexpected errors now propagate, improving debuggability.

2. **Added PEP 257 docstrings** to three functions:
   - _to_decimal(): Explains Decimal conversion for monetary values
   - _parse_expiry(): Explains date parsing with expected format
   - _validate_item(): Explains item validation constraints
   - Standard format: one-line summary, Args, Returns, Raises sections

- ruff: ✅ 0 violations
- mypy: ✅ 0 type errors
- pytest: ✅ 25/25 tests passed (100% success rate)
